name: Kaab ibn Jabir
title: Killer of Burayr
shortDescription: One of Umar Sa'd's soldiers who cowardly martyred Burayr ibn Khudayr (the great Quran reciter of Kufa).
fullStory:
Ka'b ibn Jabir ibn Amr was one of the vile and blind-hearted forces in the Kufan army present in Umar ibn Sa'd's camp in Karbala.

On the day of Ashura, when Burayr ibn Khudayr (the master of Kufan reciters and a great companion of the Imam) came to the battlefield and engaged with 'Radi ibn Munqidh', Burayr managed to throw Radi to the ground and sat on his chest to dispatch him.

At this moment, Ka'b ibn Jabir came from behind to help his comrade and cowardly struck Burayr in the back with a spear. While gripping Radi's teeth, Burayr was martyred by Ka'b ibn Jabir's sword strike.

Ka'b's wife (or sister), who was a believing woman, found out upon his return to Kufa that he was the killer of Burayr. Angrily, she told Ka'b: 'Did you kill the master of reciters? I will never speak a word to you for the rest of my life!' and disowned him forever.
quoteArabic: أَعَنْتَ عَلى فاطِمَةَ، وَقَتَلْتَ سَيِّدَ القُرّاءِ! لا كَلَّمْتُكَ مِنْ رَأْسِي كَلِمَةً أَبَداً...
quote: (His wife's anger towards him:) You went to war against Fatima's son and killed the master of reciters? I will never speak to you again...
